dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 1703 RH: Choices for Increased Mobility Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Joyce of Pennsylvania (for himself and Mr. Buchanan) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned
Additional sponsors: Ms. Schrier, Mr. Meuser, Mr. Thompson of Pennsylvania, Mr. Moulton, Mr. Bresnahan, Ms. Norton, Ms. Gillen, Mr. Van Orden, and Mr. Van Drew
Reported from the Committee on Energy and Commerce with an amendment
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic

action.action-desc: Committee on Ways and Means discharged; committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on February 27, 2025

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to clarify payment rules for manual wheelchairs under part B of the Medicare program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Choices for Increased Mobility Act of 2026.

section.header: Special rules related to treatment of certain manual wheelchairs under the Medicare program
section.text: Section 1834(a) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395m(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Special rules related to treatment of certain manual wheelchairs

subparagraph.header: Requirement to establish separate HCPCS codes for certain manual wheelchairs
subparagraph.text: With respect to ultralightweight manual wheelchairs payable under this subsection furnished on or after January 1, 2028, the Secretary shall establish two or more HCPCS codes, as determined appropriate by the Secretary, for the base of such a wheelchair depending on the construction material used in such base (with 1 or more such codes for such a base with titanium or carbon fiber construction material and 1 or more such codes for such a base without such materials).

clause.text: On or after January 1, 2028, in the case where an individual purchases or rents from a supplier an ultralightweight manual wheelchair that has titanium or carbon fiber construction material used in the base of such wheelchair, payment to the supplier shall be made in the frequency and amount as would otherwise be made under this subsection (as described in paragraph (1)(A)) for such wheelchair.
The supplier may charge the individual the difference between the supplier's actual charge for such wheelchair and the payment amount described in clause (i) for such wheelchair.

In order to inform an individual of the individual’s potential financial liability under clause (ii), the Secretary may require a supplier to issue a notice to the individual (in a form and manner determined by the Secretary) prior to the purchase or rental of such ultralightweight manual wheelchair by such individual.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported from the Committee on Energy and Commerce with an amendment
Committee on Ways and Means discharged; committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union and ordered to be printed
